---
title: "Kiselev — Russian Classical Track"
subtitle: "A. P. Kiselev's textbooks, chapter-by-chapter — the spine of Russian school mathematics for over a century"
---

## Why this track exists

The seven synthesis tiers of *Applied Maths* and the CBSE / NCERT board
track give two complete paths through school mathematics. Neither
follows the **classical Russian deductive line** — the line that runs
from Euclid through Bhāskara and lands, for the modern student, in the
textbooks of **Andrei Petrovich Kiselev (1852–1940)**.

Kiselev's *Arithmetic*, *Algebra*, and *Geometry* trained sixty
million Soviet students between 1884 and 1955. They were the books in
the satchels of Andrey Kolmogorov, Israel Gelfand, and every Russian
Fields medallist of the twentieth century. CLAUDE.md names Kiselev
explicitly as a touchstone for *proof exposition* and *problem-first
sequencing* — this track is where the touchstone becomes a path you
can walk.

## What this track is for

Use this track if you want:

- **The classical, rigorous, deductive presentation.** Every theorem
  proved. Definitions stated once and used precisely. Diagrams that
  carry weight, not decoration.
- **Terse prose.** Kiselev never says in three paragraphs what one
  diagram and one line of reasoning can settle. If you find most modern
  textbooks padded, this is your shelf.
- **Problem-first introduction of every idea.** A new technique
  arrives via a small worked example — *Discover* before *Name* — and
  is then stated as a theorem.
- **A second view alongside the synthesis lessons.** Each Kiselev
  lesson links to a **sister synthesis lesson** in the t-tiers or the
  CBSE track, so you can compare classical proof with modern intuition.

## Books covered

```
Algebra, Part I  →  Algebra, Part II  →  Geometry: Planimetry  →  Geometry: Stereometry
```

| Tier | Book | Source |
|------|------|--------|
| `ka1` | [**Algebra, Part I**](../lessons/tier-kiselev-1-algebra-1/index.qmd) | Russian original (public domain) — [archive.org scan](https://archive.org/details/1_kiselev_algebra) |
| `ka2` | [**Algebra, Part II**](../lessons/tier-kiselev-2-algebra-2/index.qmd) | Russian original (public domain) — [archive.org scan](https://archive.org/details/kiselyov-algebra-part-2-2005) |
| `kg1` | [**Geometry, Book I — Planimetry**](../lessons/tier-kiselev-3-planimetry/index.qmd) | Givental's English translation, Sumizdat — [free PDF on cimat.mx](https://www.cimat.mx/ciencia_para_jovenes/bachillerato/libros/kiselev/%5Bkiselev%5Dbook1_planimetry.pdf) |
| `kg2` | [**Geometry, Book II — Stereometry**](../lessons/tier-kiselev-4-stereometry/index.qmd) | Givental's English translation, Sumizdat — [free PDF on cimat.mx](https://www.cimat.mx/ciencia_para_jovenes/bachillerato/libros/%5BKiselev,Givental%5DKiselev's_Geom2.pdf) |

*Arithmetic* is out of scope for this track — the synthesis Tier 1
covers the same ground in a self-study idiom.

## How a Kiselev lesson is structured

The **same six-beat contract** as every other lesson in this repo
(*Touch*, *Wonder*, *Discover*, *Name*, *Apply*, *Connect*) plus two
Kiselev-specific additions:

- **Inside *Apply*:** a short **Kiselev-style problems** block —
  three problems adapted from Kiselev's own exercise sets, in his
  terse style, with full graduated hints and worked solutions.
- **Inside *Connect*:** a **Kiselev chapter alignment** block — the
  source book, chapter, and section numbers, plus a link to the
  sister synthesis lesson for the same idea.

See [CLAUDE.md](https://github.com/rajeshpillai/applied-maths/blob/main/CLAUDE.md)
for the full authoring contract.

## How to read this track

- **Read alongside the source PDF**, not instead of it. The PDFs are
  free; this track adds the *Touch* widget, the *Wonder* questions,
  the applied-domain connections, and the self-study scaffolding
  Kiselev's compactness leaves to the teacher.
- **Compare with the sister synthesis lesson** when an idea feels
  alien. Two angles on the same idea is the whole point of having
  multiple tracks.
- **Take Kiselev's brevity seriously.** If a definition seems short,
  re-read it. Every word is doing work.

## Pilot status

This track is in **pilot phase**. Four lessons — one per book — are
built end-to-end against the contract:

- [`ka1.signed-numbers`](../lessons/tier-kiselev-1-algebra-1/01-signed-numbers/index.qmd)
- [`ka2.complex-numbers`](../lessons/tier-kiselev-2-algebra-2/01-complex-numbers/index.qmd)
- [`kg1.parallels`](../lessons/tier-kiselev-3-planimetry/04-parallels/index.qmd)
- [`kg2.cylinder-cone`](../lessons/tier-kiselev-4-stereometry/03-cylinder-cone/index.qmd)

Once the pilots have been walked through and the pattern validated, the
remaining chapters of each book will be authored chapter-by-chapter.
